sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.59 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: For a finite group$G$, let$_1(G)$denote the totality of the degrees of all irre\-du\-cible complex characters of$G$with allowance for their multiplicities. Suppose that$H$is a finite group with$_1(H)= _1(G)$. [r]{a)} If$G$is an almost simple group, must$H$be isomorphic to$G$? [r]{b)} If$G$is a quasisimple group, must$H$be isomorphic to$G$? This is true if$G$is a simple group (see~11.8(a) in Archiv...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
